channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Angus Taylor commits to expanding Australia’s fuel stockpiles to 60 days
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor has committed to increasing Australia's fuel stockpiles to a minimum of 60 days by the end of his first term if the Coalition wins the 2027 election. Taylor, who previou...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Approves Public Attendance for All PSL Playoff Matches with Travel Advisory
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has officially approved the attendance of fans at all three Pakistan Super League (PSL) playoff matches, as announced by Interior Minister and PCB Chairman Mohsin Naqvi. ...

item.title: Met Office warns of rising temperatures and mild heatwave in southern Pakistan from April 29
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a heatwave alert, warning that temperatures are expected to increase notably in several regions over the coming days. According to the advisory,...

item.title: Shell to Acquire ARC Resources in $16.4 Billion Deal to Expand Shale Gas Production
item.description: British energy company Shell don announce say dem go buy Canadian energy firm ARC Resources for $16.4 billion. This acquisition go give Shell better access to shale gas and liquids production for Cana...

item.title: Spotify Expands Into Fitness Content with Peloton Partnership
item.description: Spotify has announced its expansion into the fitness sector, marking its next major content category after podcasts, audiobooks, video, and physical books. The platform aims to become a central hub fo...

item.title: Zanzibar plans national airline to improve direct international flights and tourism
item.description: Zanzibar government dey plan to start its own national airline to make tourism easier and more direct. Minister for Labour and Investment, Shariff Ali Shariff, talk say the main problem for tourism be...

item.title: Suspect in White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident set for court appearance
item.description: A suspect identified as Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old teacher and video game developer from Torrance, California, is scheduled to appear in court on Monday following a shooting incident at the White...

item.title: State Bank Hikes Key Policy Rate to 11.5% Amid Rising Global Energy Prices
item.description: The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) on Monday raised its key policy rate to 11.5 per cent, marking the first hike in almost three years. This decision comes in response to rising oil prices caused by the...

item.title: Dutch Influencer Jade Kops Passes Away at 19 after Battling Rare Cancer
item.description: Sad news has hit the online community as Dutch influencer Jade Kops has passed away at only 19 years old after a prolonged fight with rhabdomyosarcoma, a rare and aggressive cancer affecting soft tiss...

item.title: Mali Faces Escalating Security Crisis Following Minister's Death and Rebel Attacks
item.description: Things don really scatter for Mali, as coordinated attacks by rebels and jihadists don lead to the death of Defence Minister Sadio Camara and other key people, including his wife and grandchildren. Th...

item.title: West Suburban Peace Coalition Hosts Educational Forum on U.S.-Iran Relations
item.description: The West Suburban Peace Coalition is hosting an educational forum on Monday, May 4, from 7:00 to 8:00 PM Central Standard Time, focusing on the dynamics of the potential conflict between the U.S. and ...

item.title: Trump Unharmed After Incident at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: During the annual White House Correspondents’ Association dinner, a man armed with a shotgun, handgun, and knives attempted to approach President Donald Trump, who was attending for the first time as ...

item.title: Sindh Govt to Reconstruct Karachi's University Road Independently in 90 Days
item.description: Karachi residents have been facing serious difficulties due to delays in the construction of the Red Line Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) along University Road. Sindh Chief Minister Syed Murad Ali Shah, while...

item.title: Financial Adviser Explains How to Avoid Overpaying Your Mortgage
item.description: Many homeowners often make the mistake of overpaying on their mortgages without realising it may not always be in their best financial interest. The 'never saw it' rule, highlighted by a financial adv...

item.title: Othman Masoud Othman Calls for Fair Revenue Sharing in Tanzania Union to Boost Zanzibar Economy
item.description: ACT Wazalendo Chairman, Othman Masoud Othman, has strongly criticised the current Union structure between Tanganyika and Zanzibar. He says it deprives the Isles of significant revenue and weakens its ...

item.title: Balancing Media Freedom and Business Survival in Tanzania
item.description: The article addresses the complex relationship between media freedom and the need for financial sustainability in Tanzanian media houses. It discusses how the pressure to remain financially viable can...

item.title: Obama Comments on White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting Motive Amid Public Debate
item.description: Former President Barack Obama sparked controversy online after posting on X about the White House Correspondents' Dinner (WHCD) shooting, emphasizing the need to reject political violence despite ackn...

item.title: Kano State House of Assembly Confirms Murtala Sule Garo as Deputy Governor After Screening
item.description: The Kano State House of Assembly don finally confirm Murtala Sule Garo as the new Deputy Governor of Kano State. This one happen during their plenary session wey the Speaker, Jibril Isma’il Falgore, l...

item.title: Canada’s Last Living Constitution Framer Critiques Federal Handling of the Charter
item.description: Brian Peckford, former premier of Newfoundland and the last living individual who participated in the creation of the Constitution Act, 1982, has voiced concerns regarding the federal government's int...

item.title: Senator Lacson to present progress report to resume Senate flood control hearings
item.description: Senator Panfilo “Ping” Lacson, who is also the Senate President Pro Tempore and Blue Ribbon Committee Chairman, announced that he will present a progress report on the flood control project investigat...
